programmes.show_description: The Butcher of Suburbia: The landmark series brings us the gripping inside story of the murder of a 74-year-old woman - and the killer's macabre efforts to conceal her death. (Ep1/2)
The Butcher of Suburbia: The suspect admits to the murder of a 74-year-old woman. But a deeply disturbing tale that shocks experienced detectives has only just begun. (Ep2/2)
This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.
From open marriages to late-life romance, welcome to a night of secrets, surprises and second chances at the nation's most romantic restaurant.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Sue and Richard's beautiful home in Mapperley is no longer suitable since Richard had a cycling accident. Can Kirstie make it accessible? Or should they list it?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with young couple Wendy and Simon in Derbyshire and their oldest ever house-hunters, Margot and Henry, in Berkshire.
Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj throw themselves into the restoration of a derelict chapel in Pwllheli. But as they explore their new home, it seems they're not alone...
In South Wales, farm girl Claire treats her guests to a traditional, hearty Welsh spread and farm-themed games. Will her farm charm impress? Or will her night end up a mucky mess? (Ep1/5)
Fiona and Michael kick off this week's competition at their homely country pub in Norfolk. But a problem with a shower makes a guest feel dour.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: On the Costa Blanca, Abi and Darren plan a disco revolution. In France, will juggling a working business and renovations prove too much for restaurateurs Andrew and Richard?

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programmes.show_description: Chris and Kay are searching for the perfect retirement home to share with their cats, but can't agree on where it should be. Will they pick Cardigan Bay in west Wales or Spain's Mar Menor?

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Jasmine Harman revisits dad and daughter Steven and Stephanie in Charente. Laura Hamilton's with Neelesh and Angie, who bought an apartment in Estepona in 2019.
Swindon. International DJ Mark hosts a rave-themed evening, with an Ibiza-inspired menu including 'big fish little fishcakes' and 'Bora Bora Beach brownies'. (Ep1/5)
Swindon. Amateur dramatics fan Michaela plans a glamorous evening with a full band accompaniment for her guests to sing show tunes. There's just one problem: she's not a great cook. (Ep2/5)
Swindon. The joker of the group, financial advisor Scott, hopes to blow away the competition with a Wild West theme, including his unique style of country rapping. (Ep3/5)
Swindon. Sales manager Sally hosts a 'girls night in', with a cryptic menu that leaves her guests with no idea what they're about to eat. (Ep4/5)
Swindon. Alternative therapist Gill has big plans for an 'upstairs downstairs' Edwardian evening, featuring sophisticated food, followed by circle dancing to Bob Marley. (Ep5/5)
Dee and Joe have decided to swap Ireland for Portugal and make a permanent move to their favourite town of Albufeira. Jean Johansson shows them what their £155k can buy.
Kirstie and Phil catch up with cautious first-time buyers Sundeep and Rupreet in Harrow and postcode-picky Hilary and Rob, who set their hearts on Hitchin in Hertfordshire.
Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj throw themselves into the restoration of a derelict chapel in Pwllheli. But as they explore their new home, it seems they're not alone...

programmes.show_name: Rhod Gilbert: The Book of John
programmes.show_description: Raw, personal and brutally honest, award-winning comedian Rhod takes us on a no-nonsense journey into finding humour when you've hit rock-bottom. Recorded at the Wales Millennium Centre.
Retired artists Carole and Roger have crammed their nine-bed home with objects from around the world. Will they part with possessions to raise much-needed cash? (Ep2/3)

programmes.show_name: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programmes.show_description: Ex-navy pilot Rich has a huge 16cm tumour running from his jaw to his heart. Meanwhile, nearly all the organs in Tracy's pelvis need removal to combat her stage four bowel cancer.
A man has broken his leg on Scafell Pike. As darkness closes in, can the mountain rescue team save him from potentially life-threatening plummeting temperatures? (Ep2/4)
Andrew's sustained a severe facial injury in an accident at work. Tony's been crushed by a forklift truck. And in resus, 61-year-old John keeps having sudden blackouts. But why?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with Amy and Takie, who wanted rid of renting in London, and Vicky and Andy who hoped to combine a home with two businesses in Wiltshire.
Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj start ripping out rot at the chapel and pitch their plan to turn it into a home, pottery studio and events space to the local community.
In South Wales, Richard hosts at his pub, setting the bar high with terrific tartlets, sensational sea bass and crowd-pleasing cheesecake. Can he hit the bullseye? (Ep2/5)
Carl and Liz host the week's second visit, at their coaching inn in Cambridgeshire, where converted stables and Highland cows are on the menu (not literally).

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: On the Costa Blanca, Abi and Darren's 'boogie wonderland' is hit by technical trouble. And Gordon and Shona finally take the reins of a diving business in Marbella.

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programmes.show_description: Wendy and Pete from Cambridgeshire want to buy their first home together, but can't agree on where it should be: Great Yarmouth in Norfolk, or the countryside of Almería in Spain.

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Jean Johansson catches up with Bev and Paul in Spain, who she helped in 2018, while Danni Menzies travels back to Brittany to see how Jill and Paul are doing.
County Durham and Wearside. Personal trainer Eve worries that her haggis and black pudding tower might not be to everyone's taste. But there's a bigger problem: Ken's bad jokes. (Ep1/5)
County Durham and Wearside. Interior designer and wedding supplier Paula Thompson hopes to bring a touch of sparkle with her bling theme and belly dancing. (Ep2/5)
County Durham and Wearside. Languages student Donal goes all-out with a black-tie dinner party, complete with a string quartet and a locally sourced scallop starter. (Ep3/5)
County Durham and Wearside. Debt collector Eleri hosts a Welsh night, with a Welsh menu, a Welsh dress code and a local firefighter performing as Tom Jones. (Ep4/5)
County Durham and Wearside. Cheeky businessman Ken Brown holds a wear-a-wig-themed evening and serves up rhubarb and custard 'with a twist'. (Ep5/5)
Jasmine Harman helps Yvette and Agata look for their dream forever home near the centre of the action in the seaside town of Alvor on Portugal's Algarve, for their budget of £300k.
In London, can Phil find Sam and Krystle a house before they get hitched? Kirstie helps tall twins Matt and Chris, who are clubbing together to buy their first home.

programmes.show_name: Nando's vs KFC: Battle of the...
programmes.show_description: ...Brands: KFC and Nando's go head-to-head as Kate Quilton explores the battle for our taste buds in a fiery look at flavour, fandom and the billion-pound fight to win the chicken wars.
...Less: The Sellers family mealtimes are chaos thanks to dad Rudi's long working hours and daily post-commute supermarket trips. Will they ever get dinner on the table before 9pm?
Andrew's sustained a severe facial injury in an accident at work. Tony's been crushed by a forklift truck. And in resus, 61-year-old John keeps having sudden blackouts. But why?
(2014) World War II action with Brad Pitt. A tank commander leads his crew into the dark, bloody heart of Hitler's last stand. But there's a rookie on board the Fury. With Michael Peña.
